patch.value: Close-time completeness signal for structured resolution fields (resolution/expected/actual) — config-gated warn or require

## Problem
`validate_resolution_missing_fields` keeps creeping (269 → 272 across 2026-07-09→10) even though `close_reason` is enforced: agents close items with a reason but skip the structured triple `resolution` / `expected_result` / `actual_result` (set via `pm close --resolution/--expected/--actual` or `pm update`). pm-e9zh backfills the pre-enforcement legacy set, but nothing stops NEW closes from adding to the worklist, so the backfill target moves.

## Proposal
1. Emit a completeness line in the `pm close` envelope when any of the three structured fields is absent (always on, one short line — token-cheap, mirrors the existing next_transition hint pattern).
2. Add a governance settings key (e.g. `close.require_resolution_fields: off | warn | require`, default `warn`) so strict workspaces can hard-gate like `require_close_reason` already does.
3. Contract + docs + completion wiring per the new-flag checklist; validate check text should point at the setting.

## Acceptance criteria
- Closing without the triple prints exactly one advisory line naming the missing fields and the flags to set them; with `require`, close exits non-zero with the same recovery bundle shape as close_reason_required.
- `validate_resolution_missing_fields` stops growing on workspaces with warn/require enabled (verified in e2e fixture).
- No output change when all three fields are provided.

## Relation
related pm-e9zh (legacy backfill) — this item is the faucet, e9zh is the mop.

Advisory line on close when resolution/expected/actual missing; require mode exits non-zero with recovery bundle; e2e fixture proves drift stops under warn/require; zero output change when fields provided

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Evidence 2026-07-10: validate_resolution_missing_fields drifted 269 -> 272 (+3) in one day — the 2026-07-09 closes of pm-2ldo, pm-q6gx, pm-cu1i (all closed via close -m with strong prose close_reason but no structured resolution/expected/actual). Confirms the faucet is every routine agent close, not a legacy backlog: without a close-time signal the structured fields will keep losing to close_reason. Rate data point for prioritization: ~3/day at current agent throughput.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Faucet evidence 2026-07-10 (post PR #499): validate_resolution_missing_fields 272 -> 277. The +5 are the schema-fidelity bundle closures (pm-kfq5, pm-l6rz, pm-zazb, pm-b84u, pm-ghf1) — all closed with a substantive close_reason but no structured resolution/expected/actual triple, exactly the leak this item gates. Third consecutive pass with growth (269 -> 272 -> 277); each shipped lane adds ~5. Other buckets steady: missing_linked_paths 154, orphaned_paths 124, missing_close_reason 18 (pm-e9zh), duplicate_issue_codes 1 (pm-pivf).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Faucet evidence 2026-07-10 (post-PR#502): resolution_missing_fields 277 -> 278 (+1). The lane closed 8 items (pm-114v/l0bu/yl6c/hfg5/ol5v/6y58/z32q/w7jq) but only +1 leaked — close reasons were set on all 8; the +1 is a partial-fields close. Rate is falling versus the ~+5/lane baseline, but the faucet is still open until the close-time completeness gate ships.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Faucet evidence 2026-07-11: resolution_missing_fields HELD at 278 across PR#506's nine closes (pm-4ak1/5566/2vb2/768v/m0uc/mswi/qely/uwu0/uxmf) — first fully zero-leak lane observed (trend: +5/lane pre-#47, +1/8 on PR#502, 0/9 now). Close-time completeness discipline is landing without the gate; remaining 278 is legacy stock, so this item's value is now mostly ratchet-protection (prevent regression) + backfill, not stemming an active faucet.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Faucet trend update 2026-07-11 (post-PR#511): resolution_missing_fields HELD at 278 across 4 more lane closes (pm-s9iu/pm-0mjz/pm-o71e/pm-9qcr, all with structured resolutions) — SECOND consecutive zero-leak lane after PR#506's 9 closes. Two independent lanes now demonstrate close-time completeness is achievable by default; ratchet gate (fail on any increase above 278) is de-risked, remaining value is the legacy backfill half.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Faucet evidence 2026-07-11 (PR#512 lane): 7/7 closes leaked structured resolution fields (je50/d30l/zuw8/9ugc/qd2h/ypha/pmmv had close_reason only; resolution/expected/actual absent) — first leak after two consecutive zero-leak lanes (PR#506 0/9, PR#511 0/4). Trend 278->285, backfilled same day by maintainer-agent -> back to 278. Lane authors codex-root + maintainer-agent used pm close -m without pm update structured fields. Confirms close-time completeness gate (this item) is still the missing control: zero-leak lanes were author discipline, not tooling.
